fundamentals.name: MP Materials Corp.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MP Materials Corp., together with its subsidiaries, produces rare earth materials in the Western Hemisphere. It operates in two segments, Materials and Magnetics. The Materials segment owns and operates the Mountain Pass Rare Earth Mine and Processing facility located near Mountain Pass, San Bernardino County, California. The Magnetics segment produces magnetic precursor products, including NdPr metal; and manufactures NdFeB permanent magnets. MP Materials Corp. was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Las Vegas, Nevada.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but currently unprofitable with a high forward valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimate with EPS of $0.03 vs. $-0.04; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 118.6% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 174.6% — significant earnings surprise.
Analyst consensus is strong buy with a mean target of $80.40.
Strong liquidity with $1.74B cash and a current ratio of 7.18.
negatives: Operating margin -7.9% and profit margin -20.5% — the company is not yet profitable.
Forward P/E of 57.89x and Price/Sales of 34.31x indicate a very high valuation.
Free cash flow is negative $-174.84M TTM, suggesting cash burn.
High Beta of 1.91 indicates significant stock volatility.

news.paragraph: MP Materials is facing mixed signals, with a recent partnership with Apple noted alongside a stock price decline and geopolitical discussions around rare earth elements. Despite a strong beat on last quarter's EPS and positive analyst sentiment, the company's valuation is stretched and its operating margins remain negative. The upcoming earnings report and ongoing geopolitical factors will be key to watch.
